markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Store embeds as artifact/media/source IDs or typed URLs, never random absolute paths. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: EmbedReferenceModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: EmbedReferenceModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: Embed typed-reference validation never enforced on the document save path (only the side table CHECK)
evidence.fix: create/save validate every content_json embed block through EmbedTarget::parse_raw BEFORE commit (400 fail-closed) and sync knowledge_document_embeds AFTER commit via new replace_knowledge_document_embeds (delete-all+insert tx); block_embed_target_raw is the single canonical attrs reader shared with the projection renderer
evidence.tests: knowledge_documents_api_tests 6 passed incl. mt152_save_path_validates_and_persists_content_embeds (create+save sync, 6 dangerous targets reject pre-commit, no-embed save empties table); knowledge_document_model_tests 6 passed regression

findings.title: Embed typed-reference validation is never enforced on the document save path
findings.evidence: save_knowledge_rich_document_version (knowledge.rs:4532) stores content_json as-is and only re-extracts backlinks (api:466-496); it never extracts/validates embed blocks into knowledge_document_embeds. upsert_knowledge_document_embed (knowledge.rs:4801) is a separate API not called on save. So an image/video block with attrs.target=/abs/path or file:// is persisted unvalidated.
findings.fix: On save, project content_json embed blocks through EmbedTarget::new and upsert into knowledge_document_embeds (or reject the save), so the typed-reference law actually governs document content, not just the side table.

claim_mismatches.claim: embeds are stored as artifact/media/source ids or typed URLs, never random absolute paths
claim_mismatches.reality: true for rows in knowledge_document_embeds (Rust constructor + DB CHECK both proven), but the document own embed blocks in content_json are saved without that validation and are what projections render

missing_tests: test: saving a document whose image block carries an absolute-path src is rejected or sanitized (no test exercises content_json embed validation on save)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-152 EmbedReferenceModel requires rich-document embeds to be stored as typed artifact/media/source ids or typed http(s) URLs, never random absolute paths, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers a typed EmbedTarget law enforced both at the side-table DB CHECK and (after the adversarial-v2 fix) on the document content_json save path before commit, with real managed-PostgreSQL HTTP integration proof. The prior adversarial finding (save-path bypass) is genuinely closed.
findings: embed.rs:75-100 EmbedTarget::new is the only public constructor and fail-closed rejects absolute paths (is_absolute_path covers POSIX /, Windows drive-letter, UNC \\, and file: URLs at embed.rs:293-310), non-http url-kind values, and scheme-bearing id values; url_scheme (embed.rs:191-219) defeats tab/newline/case obfuscation so javascript:/data: cannot hide in an id field.

embed.rs:154-182 validate_block_embeds walks every is_embed() block through EmbedTarget::parse_raw and returns the first (block_id,error) so a bad target rejects the whole write; block_embed_target_raw (embed.rs:122-134) is the single canonical attrs reader shared with projection.rs:41, so the same bytes are governed by the same law on both render and save.

Save-path enforcement is real and pre-commit: knowledge_documents.rs:977-979 (save) and :654 (create) call validate_block_embeds and 400 fail-closed before db.save_knowledge_rich_document_version; this closes the adversarial-v2 MEDIUM (embeds validated only in the side table). Post-commit the side table is resynced via replace_knowledge_document_embeds (knowledge_documents.rs:1049).

storage/knowledge.rs:5580-5633 replace_knowledge_document_embeds is a real transactional delete-all-for-document + insert into the knowledge_document_embeds PostgreSQL table (mirrors backlinks sync), so the table can never drift from content; migration 0281 adds DB-level CHECK constraints rejecting absolute/UNC/file:/drive-letter ref_value and enforcing http(s) for url kind, and registers the table as authority-class in knowledge_schema_registry.

Real-PG proof exists: tests/knowledge_documents_api_tests.rs:526 mt152_save_path_validates_and_persists_content_embeds spins a real Axum server over PostgresDatabase::connect(pg.schema_url) (test:114-115), creates+saves a doc with a media-id embed (embeds_persisted==1), resaves with media-id+https-url (==2), asserts 6 dangerous targets (javascript:/JaVa\tScRiPt:/data:/C:\/etc/passwd/file:) all 400 fail-closed with doc_version unchanged (test:608-651), and a no-embed save empties the table (test:653-676). This touches the real Handshake-owned PG resource, not an implementer mock.

Self-cert not present: lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=null, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION. No deferred-live escapes (no todo!/unimplemented!/*Unavailable) reachable from the save path; the test SKIP guard is a managed-PG-availability skip, not a live-path placeholder.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): the MT cites spec anchor 2.3.13.11 (v02.192); exact-section resolution against current v02.194 is Integration Validator scope. Conceptual alignment with §2.3.13 storage traits + Raw/Derived/Display (CX-100) + local-first PostgreSQL authority is clear and uncontradicted.
evidence_checked: git commits b506d832, aa0ce3f6, bf0c62a5, dd3056f5 (show --stat)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
